name: Make Eurail's own pages findable by AI search engines
why: Cheapest opportunity in the set. Closes the AI search visibility gap (38/100), the lowest-scoring dimension in the maturity scorecard. Cost of not doing it is asymmetric — every week without it, third-party services accumulate citation authority on Eurail's brand-defining queries.

name: A pass-economics calculator that does the math surrogates can't
why: The wedge unique to Eurail — answers "is the pass worth it for my trip?", the question third-party surrogates approximate badly. Within Eurail's perimeter to ship.

name: An AI-callable interface for pass logic (MCP / ChatGPT App)
why: The most visible win. Puts Eurail's pass logic on the agent-callable surface (MCP servers, ChatGPT Apps) where SNCF Connect already ships and Eurail is absent.

name: A new multi-country trip planner (replaces the May 2026 sunset)
why: Replaces what the May 2026 trip-planner sunset took down, before DB Navigator's pan-European launch inherits the use case. TEL TSI regulated feeds reduce the operator dependency.

name: A retrieval-grounded customer assistant (replaces the public chatbot)
why: The lowest-uncertainty win in the set: extend the internal Zendesk AI that already works (82% satisfaction) to the public surface. Closing the contradiction is engineering work, not strategy.

name: Cross-operator disruption rebooking (the federation moat)
governance: Eurail alone on regulated feeds + few operators
why: The highest-moat capability in the diagnosis. Trainline solves disruption within UK rail, DB Navigator within DB, SNCF Connect within SNCF — none touch the cross-operator-with-pass-rules case. Only Eurail is positioned to, and TEL TSI made the data substrate available without 35-way negotiation.

name: Post-trip closure (auto delay-comp + recap + did-the-pass-pay-off?)
why: Moves the post-trip relationship back from third-party recap tools to Eurail — auto delay-compensation, a trip recap, and the "did the pass pay off?" close.

name: Federated trust infrastructure (signed audit log + provenance + special-category track)
why: The gate that unblocks all the others. Every model call, retrieval, tool invocation, and customer-facing message written to an append-only signed log, with verifiable provenance so customers can tell authentic messages from phishing — the trust bar the post-breach environment demands.
